_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Establishing long-range objectives and specifying the strategies and actions to achieve them.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in developing and executing multi-year data and AI strategies, including vision articulation, milestone definition, resource planning, and adaptive replanning in response to organizational changes.
comments: Very strong match. WIDAI LS-S-001 is a direct domain-specific expression of this activity. Also: LS-K-001 (strategic planning knowledge), LS-T-001 (strategy development task). WIDAI covers strategic planning extensively for data/AI.
_comment_atlas_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: Moderate similarity (nist band: roughly as similar as dissimilar). The moderate STS score for an Equal relationship reflects vocabulary divergence between O*NET's general-purpose language and WIDAI's domain-specific terminology. The concepts are equivalent despite surface-level phrasing differences that compress the cross-encoder score.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: F1 of 0.8790 indicates moderate token-level semantic alignment. Precision exceeds Recall, indicating that the FDE's semantic content is more tightly aligned with the KSA than the reverse — the KSA contains concepts or specificity beyond what the FDE covers. The P/R asymmetry for an Equal relationship suggests one description is more specific or uses more distinctive vocabulary than the other, even though the underlying competency is judged equivalent.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: Neutral-dominant (98.01%) — the model finds the descriptions related but neither implying nor contradicting each other. Neutral for an Equal pair reflects vocabulary divergence rather than conceptual divergence — the NLI model processes surface language, and cross-domain terminology differences suppress the entailment signal even when the underlying concepts align.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: The bi-encoder score (0.5349) meets or exceeds the STS cross-encoder (0.3755). This is uncommon and suggests the semantic similarity is well-represented in the embedding space — the descriptions occupy similar vector-space regions even without joint processing.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Low lexical overlap (9.38% of tokens shared). Some shared vocabulary exists (likely common functional words or broadly-used domain terms) but the bulk of each description uses distinct terminology.
